description.content: The Everhour API is a RESTful JSON interface for time tracking, timesheets, timers, projects, tasks, clients, invoices, expenses, scheduling, time off, and reporting. Authenticate every request with an X-Api-Key header carrying the API key from the bottom of your Everhour profile page.
Returns time records across the whole team, optionally filtered by date range and paginated. Pass opts_include_billing=1 to include billing data (admins with billing permissions only).
Returns time records reported by a specific user.
Returns time records reported against a specific task.
Returns time records reported against a specific project.
Adds a time record to a task for a user on a given date.
Updates an existing time record.
Deletes a time record.
Starts a timer on a task for the current user.
Returns the currently running timer for the current user.
Stops the currently running timer and records the tracked time.
Returns all currently running timers across the team.
Returns timecards for the whole team within a date range (last two weeks by default).
Returns timecards for a user within a date range (last two weeks by default).
Returns the timecard for a user on a specific date.
Updates clock-in, clock-out, or break time on a timecard.
Deletes a user's timecard for a specific date.
Clocks a user in, creating or updating today's timecard.
Clocks a user out on today's timecard.
Returns weekly timesheets for a user (most recent weeks first).
Returns timesheets for the whole team for a given week. The week ID is the two-digit year plus the week number (for example 2535 for week 35 of 2025).
Submits a weekly timesheet for approval (or approves it directly when called by an admin). The timesheet ID is the user ID concatenated with the week ID.
Approves or rejects a pending timesheet approval request, optionally per day of the week.
Discards a previously submitted timesheet approval request.
Returns projects, optionally filtered by name query or source integration platform.
Creates a native Everhour project.
Returns a single project by ID.
Updates a project.
Deletes a project.
Archives or unarchives a project.

Updates a project's billing type (non_billable, hourly, fixed_fee), rate configuration (project_rate or user_rate with per-user overrides), and budget (money, time, or costs; general, monthly, weekly, or daily periods).

Instantly synchronizes a project from a connected integration (Asana, Trello, ClickUp, GitHub, and more) into Everhour instead of waiting for background sync. Safe to call multiple times; returns the existing project if already synced.
Returns the sections of a project.
Creates a section in a project.
Returns a single section by ID.
Updates a section.
Deletes a section.
Returns the tasks of a project, paginated up to 250 per page.
Creates a task in a project section.
Searches tasks across all projects by name.
Searches tasks within a single project by name.
Returns a single task by ID. Pass opts_include_billing=1 to include billing data (admins with billing permissions only).
Updates a task, including custom field values.
Deletes a task.
Sets or updates the time estimate on a task (overall or per user).
Removes the time estimate from a task.
Returns resource planner assignments, filterable by type, project, task, client, and date range.
Creates a resource planner assignment. With type time-off (plus timeOffType, timeOffPeriod, and approval fields) this same endpoint creates time off for a user.
Updates a resource planner assignment.
Deletes a resource planner assignment.
Returns the team's time off types (vacation, sick leave, and so on).
Creates a time off type.
Updates a time off type.
Deletes a time off type.
Returns time off allocations (accrual balances) for team members.
Creates a time off allocation for one or more users.
Updates a time off allocation.
Deletes a time off allocation.
Returns clients, optionally filtered by name query.
Creates a client and optionally links projects to it.
Returns a single client by ID.
Updates a client.
Sets or updates a client budget - money, time, or costs based, with general, monthly, weekly, or daily periods, thresholds, and overbudget protection.
Removes the budget from a client.
Returns all invoices.
Returns a single invoice by ID.
Updates invoice details, tax, discount, and line items.
Deletes an invoice.
Creates an invoice for a client from uninvoiced time and expenses within a date range.
Rebuilds the invoice line items from current time and expense data.

item.name: Mark invoice as draft, sent, or paid

request.name: Mark invoice as draft, sent, or paid

description.content: Transitions an invoice between draft, sent, and paid statuses.

item.name: Export invoice to Xero, QuickBooks, or FreshBooks

request.name: Export invoice to Xero, QuickBooks, or FreshBooks

description.content: Exports the invoice to a connected accounting integration.
Returns all expenses.
Creates an expense in a category, optionally linked to a project and user.
Updates an expense.
Deletes an expense.
Returns all expense categories.
Creates an expense category, optionally unit-based (for example mileage).
Updates an expense category.
Deletes an expense category, optionally moving or removing its expenses.
Returns aggregated time, billable amounts, costs, profit, and expense metrics per project. Time columns are in seconds and amounts in cents.
Returns aggregated time, billable amounts, costs, profit, and expense metrics per client.
Returns aggregated time, time off, billable amounts, costs, and profit metrics per team member.
Returns the user profile that owns the API key.
Returns all team members with roles and statuses.

Creates a webhook subscription. Everhour sends a verification POST with an X-Hook-Secret header to the target URL; the target must echo the header back to complete verification. Supported events cover project, task, timer, time, section, client, and estimate changes.
Returns a webhook subscription by ID.
Updates a webhook subscription (re-triggers target URL verification).
Deletes a webhook subscription.
